lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Hello.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Hello there! Are you interested in hearing all about crafting?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I train my crafting?

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: To get started, you might like to try your hand at crafting some armour from cow's leather. There's a tanner just over in Al Kharid who could tan any hides for you.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Once you've managed to get your hand on some kind of leather, you just need a needle and some thread to get to work!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: If that doesn't take your fancy, you could try your hand at spinning wool right here on the spinning wheel.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Come to think of it... Farmer Fred, just north of here, sounds like he could use a hand shearing some of his sheep. Maybe you could help him out, and get some practice while you're at it.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How can I train my crafting?

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Lots of budding craftsmen seem to spend their time here spinning Flax into bow strings on the wheel, if that takes your fancy.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Or you might like to try your hand at crafting some armour from cow's leather. There's a tanner just over in Al Kharid who could tan any hides for you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you want to know?

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Now that you've got a little experience under your belt, the world's your oyster!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: I can't help but feel like I should be the one asking you! At this point, the world's your oyster!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: If you wanted to try your hand at making some jewellery, you'll want to get your hand on some gold and some kind of gem, with a mould to work with and a chisel at hand.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: But if that's not your thing, maybe you'd like to try making some more armour!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Cow's leather's a good place to start, but you can try all other kinds of hides, like a dragon's!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Glass blowing seems to be a popular trade, too. You can forge molten glass from some sand and soda ash, then make all kinds of things using a glass blowing pipe.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Thanks!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you want to know

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What kinds of things can be crafted?

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: All kinds of things, really! You can make armour from leather, make some pottery, try your hand at glass blowing, make some jewellery, weapons even...

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: What would you like to hear about?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about crafting armour.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Sure thing! Most armour you can craft just involves taking a needle and some thread to whatever material you can get your hands on.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: You might like to try with different kinds of animal hide, from cows, yaks, snakes, dragons... Or any other sort of fabric you can find!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Some crafty types have started making some leather- covered wooden shields recently, too!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: You might find some creatures make for a pretty sturdy helmet, too. The best way to make something like that's just to take a good old chisel to it!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: That sounds great.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Would you like to hear about anything else I mentioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about pottery.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Of course! Pottery's all kind of the same, it just takes a lot of getting used to.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: All that there really is to it is getting your hands on some clay, getting it wet and getting to work on a potter's wheel.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Once you've got the shape you want out of it, you want to put it in a pottery oven to fire it up.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: The barbarians west of Varrock seem to be pretty keen on their pottery. I'd take a look around there, if you wanted to try it out!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Once you get the hang of it, you can make pots, pie dishes, bowls... You know.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Neat!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Would you like to hear about anything else I mentioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about glass blowing.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Glass blowing? Well, to get started you'll need to get yourself some molten glass. You can make some for yourself by heating sand and soda ash in a furnace.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Once you've got a hold of that, you'll want a glass blowing pipe to blow it out in to shape, before it cools down.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: It's not the easiest thing to get to grips with, but it shouldn't be too difficult!

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Nifty.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Would you like to hear about anything else I mentioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about making jewellery.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Sure. All it boils down to is a bar of gold or silver, some kind of cut gem if you're feeling fancy, and a mould to help shape the thing.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: You can use a chisel to make a gem really shine and sparkle. You wouldn't want to use an uncut one for any jewellery.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Once you've got all of your materials together, you'll want to use a furnace to actually craft whatever it is you're trying to make!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Wizards seem to like enchanting their jewellery to do all kinds of things, too, but I don't know a lot about that.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Sounds good!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Would you like to hear about anything else I mentioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Tell me about crafting weapons.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Well, there aren't too many weapons that people tend to craft, but there are a couple worth noting!

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Battlestaves are probably the most common. It boils down to fastening an orb of some kind onto the end of a battlestaff.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: You do need to get the orb in the first place, though... You can make one through glass blowing, but it's not much use without being charged.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: I can't help much with crafting it, though! There's a little more magic involved there than I'm familiar with.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Aside from battlestaves, there are a couple of weapons that people like to make from silver... Sickles and bolts, usually.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Interesting...

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Would you like to hear about anything else I mentioned?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Not right now.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Is there anything else you want to know?

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: No, thank you.

lines.speaker: Crafting tutor
lines.text: Well, just come back any time you want to know anything!
